questions.text: I find satisfaction in perfecting a process until it runs smoothly every time.
I often take it upon myself to resolve group disagreements by choosing a path.
I notice I carry physical tension that maps directly onto whatever I'm feeling emotionally.
My reactions to sudden change are swift and visceral.
I find that I can discuss the most difficult aspects of the human experience without losing my analytical perspective.
I believe that the pull toward acting on behalf of those who are suffering is the most trustworthy moral signal I can follow.
I believe that the obligation to respond to suffering should override competing pressures whenever the two come into conflict.
I enjoy doing meaningful work that does not require being the public face.
I notice when someone leans slightly away from a person they distrust.
I often make purchases on impulse when something catches my eye.
I tend to allocate the majority of my time to tasks that benefit other people.
I sense when someone is performing confidence they do not actually feel.
I routinely turn down favors that would distract from my personal priorities.
I tend to be unaware of power dynamics playing out in conversations.
I often establish priorities for the group without being asked.
I feel that my organizational systems give me a sense of calm and mastery.
I rarely feel the need to decompress emotionally after social interactions.
I would postpone my own goals indefinitely if someone close to me needed my help.
